title: HIV Treatment Cascade Gaps And 95-95-95 Target Tracking In Sub-Saharan Africa
summary: Build a verified workflow for measuring the HIV treatment cascade at sub-national scale in Sub-Saharan Africa, linking HIV testing data, treatment-initiation records, and viral-load-suppression data from national programs and PEPFAR-supported sites.
why_it_matters: UNAIDS 95-95-95 targets call for 95% of people living with HIV knowing their status, 95% on treatment, and 95% virally suppressed by 2025. Sub-Saharan Africa has the world's largest treatment programs, but sub-national cascade measurement is inconsistent — PHIA surveys provide gold-standard estimates every 3-5 years while routine program data is available monthly but incomplete. Reconciling survey and program data at district scale is the missing link.

success_metrics.target: Every cascade estimate links survey, program, and viral-load data with methodology.

review_policy.merge_gate: Schema validation, domain review, safety review, replication.
